channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study Suggests Positive Travel Experiences May Support Healthy Aging
item.description: A recent study by researchers at Edith Cowan University highlights the potential health benefits of travel beyond leisure. By applying the theory of entropy to tourism, the study suggests that positiv...

item.title: Indian Pilot Group Calls for Detailed Investigation into 2025 Air India Crash
item.description: A group of Indian pilots, represented by the Federation of Indian Pilots (FIP), has formally requested the aviation ministry to conduct a more thorough investigation into last year's Air India crash t...

item.title: Allegheny County Implements Payments for Incarcerated Individuals to Support Safety and Reentry
item.description: Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, has launched a policy to provide cash payments to people held in the Allegheny County Jail, a program that has sparked national attention and local debate. The payments...

item.title: Rebel Wilson and Ramona Agruma announce birth of second daughter
item.description: Australian actress Rebel Wilson and her wife, fashion designer Ramona Agruma, have welcomed their second child, just days after Wilson concluded her testimony in a high-profile defamation trial in Syd...

item.title: Search Suspended for Missing Australian Woman in Cape Breton Highlands National Park
item.description: The RCMP have suspended the search for Denise Ann Williams, a 62-year-old Australian woman who went missing in Cape Breton Highlands National Park near Chéticamp, Nova Scotia. Williams was reported mi...

item.title: AD Thomas Secures Victory in Alappuzha in Kerala Assembly Elections
item.description: Kerala recently witnessed the results of its Assembly elections, with AD Thomas, representing the Kerala Youth Congress, winning the Alappuzha constituency. This victory is significant as it reflects ...

item.title: How TikTok trends are subtly spreading far-right ideas
item.description: A recent analysis of TikTok content reveals how extremist narratives are being normalised through seemingly harmless videos. The so-called 'Agartha' trend, inspired by myths of a hollow Earth, mixes w...

item.title: UK government convenes cross-sector summit amid rise in antisemitic incidents
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is set to host a Downing Street summit bringing together senior figures from business, policing, culture, health, education and civil society to tackle antisemitism thr...

item.title: Trump Suggests Possibility of Serving Beyond Constitutionally Allowed Terms
item.description: During a recent small business summit, former President Donald Trump made comments suggesting he may consider remaining in office beyond the limits set by the U.S. Constitution. He referenced small bu...

item.title: Geoffrey Mosiria Proposes Digital Support and DJ Academy to Help DJ Evolve Rebuild His Career
item.description: Popular Kenyan DJ Evolve, whose real name is Felix Orinda, is back in the news after his 2020 shooting incident came up again during a recent TV interview on NTV's Fixing the Nation. Embakasi East MP ...

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Responds to Right-Wing Criticism Over Giuliani Jokes
item.description: On May 5, 2026, late-night host Jimmy Kimmel addressed criticism from right-wing media following a joke he made about former New York Mayor Rudy Giuliani, humorously depicting him as a vampire. Giulia...

item.title: 76-Year-Old Doctoral Graduate Highlights Ecological Importance of Durban’s Mangroves
item.description: Dr Denise Ingrid Adams, aged 76, has become the oldest graduate at this year’s UKZN autumn graduation, earning a PhD for research that combines environmental observation with creative artistic practic...

item.title: Rising data centre demand puts pressure on Australia’s water resources
item.description: Australia is experiencing a rapid expansion in data centres driven by the growing demand for artificial intelligence and digital services. These facilities, often as large as football stadiums, requir...

item.title: The decline of Britain’s nightlife: rising costs, changing habits and urban challenges
item.description: Britain’s nightlife sector has experienced a significant decline in recent years, with more than a quarter of late-night venues closing between 2020 and 2025. Cities such as Birmingham have been parti...

item.title: Key Impacts of the 2026 Victorian Budget on Services and Communities
item.description: Victorian Treasurer Jaclyn Symes has delivered her second budget, focusing on easing cost-of-living pressures and achieving a modest operating surplus. Public transport users will benefit from free tr...

item.title: Minister Faces Questions Over Missing Chinese-Donated SUVs
item.description: The article highlights concerns regarding Minister of Social Development Sisisi Tolashe and her handling of two Chinese-donated SUVs intended for the ANC Women's League. According to reporting by Dail...

item.title: Modern treatments and awareness are improving menopause care for women
item.description: Millions of women in Canada experience menopause, yet only a small proportion receive medical treatment for symptoms that can significantly affect their quality of life. These symptoms—such as hot fla...

item.title: Rising State-Sponsored Hostage-Taking: Emerging Research and Policy Responses
item.description: Hostage-taking by nation-states is becoming a growing international concern, moving beyond its traditional association with non-state actors like terrorist groups and criminal organisations. Countries...

item.title: Transport group accuses DOTr officials of irregular Stradcom contract extension
item.description: A transport advocacy group, Coalition 169, has lodged formal complaints against both former and current officials of the Department of Transportation (DOTr) over the alleged improper extension of Stra...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Missile and Drone Attacks on UAE, Urges Regional Stability
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has expressed Pakistan’s strong condemnation of recent missile and drone attacks on the United Arab Emirates, showing full support for the Emirati leadership and citizens...
